skill.name: creative-media
skill.description: Creative media for agents over one zero-setup Image Skill runtime: image generation, image editing, video, audio, and image-to-3D assets with no provider API key, OAuth, local runtime, or per-provider billing account. Use when an agent needs generated media plus durable hosted URLs, recoverable jobs, cost receipts, model parameters, stable JSON, payments, and feedback through one identity and one wallet.
skill.summary: Single zero-setup CLI for image, video, audio, and 3D generation through one hosted runtime and one wallet.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The skill is a 79-line documentation file describing a hosted creative media runtime accessed via the published `image-skill` npm package. All 32 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives: the 19 `external_commands` hits are legitimate `npx` and `export` bash commands in documentation code blocks, the 8 `network` hits are hardcoded URLs to the project's own canonical resources (image-skill.com), the 3 `filesystem` hits are path documentation for the skills.sh adapter, and the 2 `weak crypto` hits are false triggers on description text. No data exfiltration, credential theft, obfuscation, or prompt injection patterns were found.

low_findings.title: External CLI commands via npx
low_findings.description: The skill documents use of `npx -y image-skill@latest` and `npx skills add` commands. These are standard package invocations from a published npm package, not arbitrary command execution. Risk is low because commands are deterministic and target a known, versioned package.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded external API URLs
low_findings.description: URLs to image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com are hardcoded in the skill manifest metadata and documentation. These point to the project's own hosted runtime, which is expected behavior for a service-backed skill. No third-party exfiltration endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: Filesystem path documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documents environment variables and paths (HOME, CODEX_HOME, ~/.agents) for the skills.sh Codex adapter. These are instructional references, not actual filesystem manipulation code.

content.user_title: Generate Images, Video, and 3D with One CLI
content.value_statement: Agents need creative media but face fragmented provider APIs, key management, and billing setups. This skill provides a single zero-setup CLI that handles image, video, audio, and 3D generation through one hosted runtime and one wallet.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using the image-skill create command
List available models and capabilities with models list --available --json
Install via skills.sh with the danielgwilson/image-skill-cli slug
Use a single hosted runtime without provider API keys or local model servers
Access durable hosted URLs for generated media assets
Submit structured feedback via image-skill feedback create --json
limitations: Requires network access to image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com
Depends on the published image-skill npm package availability
Preview contract status means APIs and contracts may change
No offline or local-model execution path

use_cases.title: Quick image prototyping
use_cases.description: An agent needs to generate a product mockup image from a text description without setting up a provider account.

use_cases.title: Multimodal asset pipeline
use_cases.description: An agent pipeline needs image, video, and 3D assets generated through one consistent CLI interface with stable JSON output.
use_cases.target_user: AI pipeline engineers and automation developers

use_cases.title: Agent skill marketplace integration
use_cases.description: An agent harness using Codex or Claude Code needs a pluggable creative media skill installable via skills.sh.

prompt_templates.title: First-time guide run
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Image Skill guide command to prepare for creating a compact field camera product image: npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --guide --prompt "a compact field camera on a stainless workbench"
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner exploring the skill for the first time

prompt_templates.title: List available models
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all available Image Skill models in JSON format: npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest models list --available --json

prompt_templates.title: Install via skills.sh
prompt_templates.prompt: Install this intent skill globally for Codex: npx skills add danielgwilson/image-skill-cli --skill creative-media -g -a codex -y
prompt_templates.scenario: User setting up the skill in their agent harness

prompt_templates.title: Structured feedback on failure
prompt_templates.prompt: When Image Skill lacks a model or capability, submit feedback with: image-skill feedback create --json providing attempted_command, expected_behavior, actual_behavior, and missing_capability fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user reporting a capability gap

output_examples.input: User asks: Generate an image of a compact field camera on a stainless workbench
output: The guide command is executed via npx
Image Skill returns a hosted URL for the generated image
Stable JSON output includes model parameters and a cost receipt
The asset is stored durably for future agent recovery

output_examples.input: User asks: What models are available for creative media generation?
output: models list --available --json returns a structured array of models
Each entry includes model name, modality, and capability flags
Output is parseable JSON suitable for agent decision-making

best_practices: Always run the guide command first and wait for ready_to_create before executing spend commands
Check next_command_copy_runnable is true before running data.next_command
Use recommended_no_spend_command to verify safely when spend is not allowed
anti_patterns: Do not supply provider API keys or create separate provider accounts for this skill
Do not run data.next_command if next_command_copy_runnable is false
Do not run a local model server alongside this skill

faq.question: Does this skill require a provider API key?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses a hosted Image Skill runtime with its own token system, so no provider API key is needed.

faq.question: What AI tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as listed in the supported tools.

faq.question: How do I install the skill?
faq.answer: Run the npx skills add command with the danielgwilson/image-skill-cli slug and the creative-media skill name.

faq.question: What happens if Image Skill lacks a needed capability?
faq.answer: Use a fallback for that specific gap and submit structured feedback via image-skill feedback create --json.

faq.question: Is there an optional token I can set?
faq.answer: Yes. IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN is an optional hosted restricted token minted by image-skill signup, not a user-supplied provider credential.

faq.question: What contract status is this skill?
faq.answer: The metadata lists status as preview-contract, meaning APIs and contracts may change as the service evolves.
